subject: [PATCH] nfsd: fix posix_acl leak and ignored error in nfsd4_create_file

reviews.author: Chuck Lever
reviews.summary: Identified the posix_acl leak and ignored error in nfsd4_create_file(). Suggested applying the patch to nfsd-testing.
reviews.raw_body:
From: Chuck Lever <chuck.lever@oracle.com>

On Thu, 21 May 2026 12:37:33 -0400, Jeff Layton wrote:
> nfsd4_create_file() has two bugs in its ACL handling:
> 
> The return value of nfsd4_acl_to_attr() is silently discarded.  When
> the NFSv4-to-POSIX ACL conversion fails (e.g., -EINVAL for
> unsupported ACE types), the file is created without any ACL and the
> client receives NFS4_OK.  This violates RFC 7530/8881 which require
> the server to reject unsupported attributes on CREATE.
> 
> [...]

Applied to nfsd-testing, thanks!

[1/1] nfsd: fix posix_acl leak and ignored error in nfsd4_create_file
      commit: 15906201636c0850eff0e2e86791f24e007d4160

--
Chuck Lever <chuck.lever@oracle.com>

2026-05-21.patch_summary: This patch fixes two bugs in the NFSv4 server's ACL handling code: a posix_acl leak and an ignored error in nfsd4_create_file(). The leaks occur when start_creating() fails after populating ACL attributes, causing the posix_acl allocations to be lost. The ignored error is due to silently discarding the return value of nfsd4_acl_to_attr(), which should reject unsupported ACE types. The patch addresses these issues by capturing the return value and changing early error paths to jump to out instead of out_write.
